---
title: "List and plan features"
subtitle: "`available_features()` and `plan_features()` expose the route before execution."
sidebar: start
---

## Catalogue rows

The high-level workflow catalogue reports public feature IDs, supported modalities, required dependencies, output schemas, and whether a feature needs explicit opt-in. Use `public_only=False` when you need to inspect every registered extractor, including internal aliases used by older recipes and implementation tests.

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import available_features

rows = available_features(modality="text")
[
    {
        "feature_id": row.feature_id,
        "modalities": row.modalities,
        "output_schema": row.output_schema,
        "requires_opt_in": row.requires_opt_in,
        "is_public": row.is_public,
    }
    for row in rows
]
```

## Plan rows

Planning resolves a requested feature to the extractor name, input token, default parameters, and output schema. Nothing has run yet.

```{python}
from natural_features.workflows import plan_features

plan = plan_features(
    "The scene opens on a quiet room.",
    features=["text.tokenize"],
)
[
    {
        "feature_id": row.feature_id,
        "step_id": row.step_id,
        "use": row.use,
        "input_token": row.input_token,
        "params": row.params,
    }
    for row in plan.rows
]
```

## Refuses to

- Unknown feature IDs raise `KeyError`.
- Features whose modality cannot be routed from available inputs raise `ValueError`.
- Optional or costly features require an explicit budget opt-in.

## Read next

[Extract features](03-extract-features.qmd)
